source_paper_title: Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Okuno_2025, title={Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0298}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0298}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Okuno, Takayuki}, year={2025}, month=july }
context:
Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19.

There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot{x}(\mu)\) in the x-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \[\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot{x}(\mu) = \xi^{\ast},\] where \(\xi^{\ast}\) was defined in Proposition 5.
title: Prove tangential derivative convergence of NSDP central path as barrier vanishes
background_latex:
Consider the nonlinear semidefinite program (NSDP)
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} f(x) \quad \text{s.t. } G(x)\in \mathbb{S}^m_+,\; h(x)=0,
\]
where \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{S}^m\), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\) are twice continuously differentiable, \(\mathbb{S}^m\) is the space of real symmetric \(m\times m\) matrices, and \(\mathbb{S}^m_+\) (resp. \(\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\)) denotes the cone of positive semidefinite (resp. definite) matrices.

Let the Lagrangian be
\[
L(x,Y,z):= f(x) - G(x)\bullet Y + h(x)^\top z,
\]
where \(Y\in\mathbb{S}^m\) is the dual matrix for the semidefinite constraint, \(z\in\mathbb{R}^s\) is the multiplier for \(h(x)=0\), and \(A\bullet B:=\mathrm{trace}(AB)\).

A (primal-dual) barrier KKT (BKKT) triplet with barrier parameter \(\mu>0\) is \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\in \mathbb{R}^n\times\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\times\mathbb{R}^s\) satisfying
\[
\nabla_x L(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))=0,\qquad h(x(\mu))=0,\qquad G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I,
\]
with \(G(x(\mu))\in\mathbb{S}^m_{++}\). A central path is a smooth mapping \(\mu\mapsto w(\mu)\) of BKKT triplets for sufficiently small \(\mu>0\).

Let \(x^*\) be a KKT point of the NSDP. Under the paper's standing assumptions (strict complementarity at \(x^*\) for some multiplier, an enhanced second-order sufficient condition holding uniformly over the Lagrange multiplier set at \(x^*\), and the Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification at \(x^*\)), the paper establishes existence of a unique smooth central path \(w(\mu)\) for \(\mu\in (0,\bar\mu)\) that converges as \(\mu\downarrow 0\) to a distinguished KKT triplet \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\), where \((Y_a,z_a)\) is the analytic center of the (possibly non-singleton) Lagrange multiplier set at \(x^*\).

The paper also defines a vector \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component (when it exists) of solutions \((\Delta x,\Delta Y)\) to a certain linear system obtained by formally differentiating a symmetric form of the BKKT equations at the limit point \(w_a\). This vector \(\xi^*\) is characterized in the paper and is shown to be the limit of the scaled primal displacement \((x(\mu)-x^*)/\mu\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\). Denote by \(\dot x(\mu):=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) the tangential direction (derivative) of the central path in the primal space, defined for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) by smoothness of \(x(\cdot)\).
problem_definition_latex:
Under the assumptions ensuring existence of a unique smooth central path \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\) for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) converging to \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\), and with \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denoting the vector defined (in the paper) as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of the linearized BKKT derivative system at \(w_a\), determine whether
\[
\lim_{\mu\to 0^+} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*
\]
holds, where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\).
latex:

\[

\]

\[
\nabla_x L(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))=0,\qquad h(x(\mu))=0,\qquad G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I,

Under the assumptions ensuring existence of a unique smooth central path \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\) for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) converging to \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\), and with \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) denoting the vector defined (in the paper) as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of the linearized BKKT derivative system at \(w_a\), determine whether
\[

\]
holds, where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\).
location_in_paper: Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19.
open_problem_quote: There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \(\dot{x}(\mu)\) in the x-space as \(\mu \to 0\). We have the following conjecture: \[\lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot{x}(\mu) = \xi^{\ast},\] where \(\xi^{\ast}\) was defined in Proposition 5.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The conjecture concerns a limiting derivative along the (unique) central path, so falsification would require constructing an NSDP instance where the central path is smooth but \(\dot x(\mu)\) fails to converge or converges to a different vector. One can numerically track central paths via solving BKKT systems for many \(\mu\) and approximate \(\dot x(\mu)\), but ensuring the paper’s assumptions and ruling out numerical artifacts makes brute-force counterexample search only moderately feasible.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate instances (small-dimensional NSDPs with polynomial/affine data) and solvers can be encoded, and a fitness signal can be based on estimated deviation between \(\dot x(\mu)\) and \(\xi^*\) over a grid of small \(\mu\). However, reliably enforcing the required regularity assumptions and accurately estimating derivatives near \(\mu=0\) is delicate and computationally expensive, making automated evolutionary search plausible but not straightforward.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.

literature_review.synthesis:
Okuno (2024) proves that, under strict complementarity at a KKT point \(x^*\), MFCQ, and a uniform enhanced SOSC over the (possibly non-singleton) multiplier set \(\Lambda(x^*)\), there is a unique smooth primal-dual central path \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\) for \(\mu\in(0,\bar\mu)\) converging to the distinguished limit \(w_a=(x^*,Y_a,z_a)\), where \((Y_a,z_a)\) is the analytic center of \(\Lambda(x^*)\). A key asymptotic refinement is the first-order expansion \(x(\mu)=x^*+\mu\xi^*+o(\mu)\), where \(\xi^*\) is characterized as the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of a linear system obtained by formally differentiating a symmetric BKKT system at \(w_a\). This establishes convergence of the scaled displacement \((x(\mu)-x^*)/\mu\to\xi^*\).

The remaining open issue is whether the tangential derivative \(\dot x(\mu)\) itself converges to the same vector \(\xi^*\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\). In nonlinear programming, Wright and Orban (2002) prove the analogous statement for degenerate problems by differentiating the barrier KKT system and showing the derivative remains bounded and converges to the solution of the limiting linearized system. For linear SDP, Sturm and Zhang (2001) provide sensitivity/derivative results for central solutions under strict complementarity, complementing earlier analytic-center convergence results (Luo--Sturm--Zhang 1998). These works suggest a plausible route for NSDP: obtain uniform control of the inverse of the Jacobian of the differentiated BKKT mapping along \(w(\mu)\) and pass to the limit \(\mu\to 0\), but the nonlinear matrix constraint introduces the sigma-term curvature and multiplier-set geometry that complicate such uniform estimates.

At present, no forward-citing literature was provided that resolves Okuno’s conjecture, and the available references mainly supply templates and partial analogues (NLP and linear SDP). Thus the problem appears still open: proving \(\dot x(\mu)\to\xi^*\) likely requires strengthening the asymptotic expansion from \(x(\mu)=x^*+\mu\xi^*+o(\mu)\) to a differentiability-at-the-boundary statement for \(x(\cdot)\) at \(\mu=0\), e.g., showing \(x(\mu)=x^*+\mu\xi^*+o(\mu)\) with an error term whose quotient by \(\mu\) is differentiable or at least has vanishing derivative, or establishing that \(\dot w(\mu)\) solves a uniformly well-conditioned linear system \(A(w(\mu))\dot w(\mu)=(0,2I,0)\) whose coefficients converge suitably to the limiting system defining \(\xi^*\).

sources.title: Analysis of the primal-dual central path for nonlinear semidefinite optimization without the nondegeneracy condition
sources.theorem_or_result:
Establishes existence and uniqueness of a smooth primal-dual central path for NSDP under strict complementarity, ESOSC, and MFCQ without nondegeneracy; proves convergence of the path to the KKT point and to the analytic center of the multiplier set, and shows 
\[\lim_{\mu\downarrow 0}\frac{x(\mu)-x^*}{\mu}=\xi^*\]
where \(\xi^*\) is the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of a linearized derivative system at the limit triplet \(w_a\); conjectures tangential derivative convergence \(\dot x(\mu)\to \xi^*\).
sources.connection: This is the source paper posing the open problem: it proves scaled displacement convergence to \(\xi^*\) but leaves open whether the actual derivative \(\dot x(\mu)\) converges to the same limit as \(\mu\to 0^+\).

sources.title: Properties of the log-barrier function on degenerate nonlinear programs
sources.theorem_or_result: For nonlinear programming without LICQ, analyzes log-barrier trajectories and proves (under suitable second-order/regularity assumptions) convergence of the tangential derivative of the central path to the solution of the formally differentiated KKT system (e.g., their Theorem 12).
sources.connection: Provides the closest known analogue in standard nonlinear programming: a template for proving \(\dot x(\mu)\to \xi^*\) by differentiating the barrier KKT system and controlling the Jacobian inverse as \(\mu\downarrow 0\). Okuno explicitly cites this as the corresponding result to be generalized to NSDP.

sources.title: On sensitivity of central solutions in semidefinite programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Studies sensitivity of primal-dual central solutions in linear SDP; establishes differentiability properties and convergence of derivatives of the central path under strict complementarity and related regularity assumptions.
sources.connection: Gives linear-SDP precedent for derivative convergence of the central path; suggests that with appropriate regularity one can control the derivative \(\dot w(\mu)\) as \(\mu\downarrow 0\), but does not cover nonlinear \(G(x)\) or multiplier nonuniqueness.

sources.title: Superlinear convergence of a symmetric primal-dual path following algorithm for semidefinite programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Under strict complementarity for linear SDP, shows primal-dual central path converges to the analytic center and establishes strong local properties used to prove superlinear convergence of path-following methods.
sources.connection: Background on analytic-center limits and local behavior of SDP central paths; informs the NSDP setting where Okuno proves convergence to the analytic center of the multiplier set.

sources.title: Local and superlinear convergence of a primal-dual interior point method for nonlinear semidefinite programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Under strict complementarity, SOSC, and nondegeneracy, proves nonsingularity of the Jacobian of an equation system equivalent to BKKT conditions, yielding existence/uniqueness of a smooth central path via the implicit function theorem and local superlinear convergence of a PDIPM.
sources.connection: Represents the prior NSDP central-path theory with nondegeneracy; Okuno removes nondegeneracy but still assumes strict complementarity. Techniques about Jacobian nonsingularity and implicit-function-based differentiability are relevant to studying \(\dot x(\mu)\) limits.

sources.title: A primal–dual interior point method for nonlinear semidefinite programming
sources.theorem_or_result: Develops a primal-dual interior-point framework for NSDP based on perturbed KKT systems and scaling; provides algorithmic context and local analysis under regularity assumptions.
sources.connection: Algorithmic motivation for central-path analysis and the differentiated BKKT system; informs why tangential derivative behavior matters for Newton systems and step computation.

sources.title: On the convergence of the central path in semidefinite optimization
sources.theorem_or_result: Shows convergence of the central path in linear SDP even without strict complementarity using curve selection/algebraic geometry tools; discusses possible non-analytic-center limits when strict complementarity fails.
sources.connection: Background on what can go wrong without strict complementarity; Okuno notes strict complementarity is crucial for his \(\mu=\Theta(\|x(\mu)-x^*\|)\) estimate, which would likely also be needed to control \(\dot x(\mu)\).
